entry.title: Intercompany STO Process (Valuated Stock In-Transit) Between 2 EWM Managed Warehouses Using SPED
entry.content:
Introduction:

The Document will discuss the STO process between 2 EWM-based warehouses belonging to 2 different company codes. The process talks about the steps and configurations that are required to successfully run the process in the S/4 Managed EWM system. Since there are various documents available where movement types 643 and 101 are used to do this process, we will be dealing with valuated stock in transit i.e. the goods issue will be done via movement type 683 and the goods receipt will be done via movement type 107 and 109. We will also be automating the process of Inbound delivery via SPED output type.

One of the most important points to note here is that as we are using cross-delivery HUs hence the HU’s will be transferred from the outbound delivery to the inbound delivery if the receiving warehouse is also managed by an EWM system. 

However, this process does not work if you have activated a unique number assignment for HUs. As a best practice whenever you have multiple EWM systems in your landscape as a good practice the HU number range should be different in both warehouses.

Please read more FAQ about this in SAP note 1119073 

 

STO FLOW:

 

  1. A Purchase Order is created to move stock between two EWM-managed warehouses.
  2. Outbound delivery is generated via VL10B.
  3. Outbound delivery is distributed to EWM Automatically.
  4. Picking is done via /SCWM/PRDO or RF Device.
  5. Goods issue is reflected back in outbound delivery in S/4 landscape by creating Material document with movement type 683 and 107.
  6. SPED output type is created and processed to create inbound delivery for receiving of stock.
  7. HU which were generated during repacking are attached to inbound delivery.
  8. Inbound Delivery is gets distributed in the EWM system.
  9. Goods receipt and putaway is done in the EWM system via /SCWM/PRDI or RF Device.
  10. Goods receipt is reflected in S/4 Landscape by creating a Material document with movement type 109.

 

 


Pre-Requisites for Intercompany STO Process:

  • The HU Number range should be unique in both the warehouses.
  • The HU Number range of the 1st warehouse should be maintained as the external number range in the 2nd warehouse.
  • The HU Number range of the 2nd warehouse should be maintained as the external number range in the 1st warehouse.

 

Configuration Required:

Define Shipping Data for Plants:

SPRO –> IMG –> Material Management –> Purchasing –> Purchase order –> Set up Stock Transport order –> Define Shipping Data for Plants

 

 

Configure Delivery Type & Availability Check Procedure by Plant:

SPRO –> IMG –> Material Management –> Purchasing –> Purchase order –> Set up Stock Transport order –> Assign Delivery Type and Checking Rule

 

Assign Document Type, One-Step Procedure, under delivery Tolerance:

SPRO –> IMG –> Material Management –> Purchasing –> Purchase order –> Set up Stock Transport order –> Assign Document Type, One-Step Procedure, Under delivery Tolerance

 

Define Confirmation Control:

Check the confirmation control key 0004 (Inbound Delivery) in Customizing for Materials Management, under Purchasing  Confirmations  Set Up Confirmation Control.

Under Confirmation Sequence, the two checkboxes GR-Relevant and GR with Confirmation must be set.

Set Up Condition Type SPED:

Check output determination procedure V10000 (Header Output) in Customizing for Logistics Execution, under Shipping  Basic Shipping Functions  Output Control  Output Determination  Maintain Output Determination for Outbound Deliveries  Maintain Output Determination Procedure.

Under Control Data, there must be an entry with condition type SPED (Stock Transfer Generates Inbound Delivery) and condition 408 (Stock Transfer Create ID).

Define Delivery type:

Check confirmation category LA (Inbound Delivery) in Customizing for Materials Management, under Purchasing  Confirmations  Define Internal Confirmation Categories.

The Delivery Type Inbound Delivery Stock in Transit field must contain an inbound delivery type for the process with stock in transit. We will be using the standard delivery type ELST (Inbound Delivery Receiving Valuated Stock in Transit). 

This delivery type must use an item category that uses movement type 109 (Goods Receipt from Valuated Goods Receipt Blocked Stock) or another movement type with posting string reference 109.

 

Define Item Categories for Delivery (Inbound):

In Customizing for Logistics Execution, under Shipping in the Customizing for Deliveries use the activity Define Item Categories to Deliveries, ensure that movement type 109 or a movement type with posting string reference 109 is assigned to this item category.

 

 

In Customizing for Logistics Execution, in the Customizing activity Define Item Category Determination in Deliveries, and check the item category that is assigned to this delivery type.

 

 

 

Define Item Categories for Delivery (Outbound):

As per above mentioned configuration we are using delivery type NCCR for triggering goods issue via 683 movement type

 

Hence based on item category determination NCCR delivery item is configured to be determined.

 

Once the item category is determined relevant Schedule line category is determined 

 

Based on the movement type maintained in the schedule line category, the goods issue will be triggered.

 

Setup condition record for SPED:

The system creates inbound deliveries with this item category when processing output type SPED during goods issued for an outbound delivery.

In transaction VV22 (Change Output - Condition Records: Shipping), ensure that in output determination for output type SPED, the condition records for the outbound delivery type used (NCCR, NCC3, NLIR, or NLI3) have been created as follows:

Output type SPED with role WE (Goods Recipient) or SH (Ship to Party), medium 8 (Special Function), and time 4 (Send Immediately (After Saving Application))

 

 

Process Flow Diagram:

 

 

Testing:

1. A Purchase Order is created to move stock between two EWM-managed warehouses

 

 

2. Outbound delivery is generated via VL10B

 

 

 

 

3. Outbound delivery is distributed to EWM Automatically

 

4. Warehouse Task Created

 

 

5. Warehouse Tasks are confirmed to complete the picking process

 

 

6. Goods issue posted in EWM

 

 

7. Goods issue is reflected back in outbound delivery in S/4 landscape by creating Material document with movement type 683 and 107.

 

8. HU information updated at the S/4 level

 

9. SPED output type is created and processed to create inbound delivery for receiving of stock.

 

10. Inbound delivery is created automatically and reflected in Purchase order

 

11. HU which were generated during repacking are attached to inbound delivery

12. Inbound Delivery gets distributed in the EWM system automatically.

 

13. Goods receipt and putaway is done in EWM system via /SCWM/PRDI or RF Device

14. Goods receipt is reflected in S/4 Landscape by creating a Material document with movement type 109

 

This completes the whole end to end process of intercompany STO between 2 EWM ,managed warehouses.

 

Regards,
Kamlesh Rampal.

entry.title: Everything About SAP Idocs in S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Everything About IDocs

IDoc are used in most SAP applications for transfer of message(information) from SAP system to other systems and vice versa.

 

IDoc is simply a data container used to exchange information between any two processes that can understand the syntax and semantics of the data.

In other words, an IDoc is like a data file with a specified format which is exchanged between 2 systems which know how to interpret that data.

IDoc stands for ” Intermediate Document”

When we execute an outbound ALE or EDI Process, an IDoc is created.

In the SAP System, IDoc are stored in database. Every IDoc has a unique number(within a client).

 

 

 

 

The IDoc structure consists of 3 parts –

  1. The administration part(Control Record)- which has the type of IDoc, message type, the current status, the sender, receiver etc. This is referred to as the Control record.
  2. The application data (Data Record) – Which contains the data. These are called the data records/segments.
  3. The Status information (Status Record)- These give you information about the various stages the IDoc has passed through.

You can view an IDoc using transaction WE02 or WE05

 

 

 

As seen the screenshot above IDoc record has three parts Control, Data and Status.

 

Control Record

  • All control record data is stored in EDIDC table. The key to this table is the IDoc Number
  • It contains information like IDoc number, the direction(inbound/outbound), sender, recipient information, channel it is using, which port it is using etc.
  • Direction ‘1’ indicates outbound, ‘2’ indicates inbound.

Data Record

  • Data record contains application data like employee header info, weekly details, client details etc
  • All data record data is stored in EDID2 to EDID4 tables and EDIDD is a structure where you can see its components.
  • It contains data like the IDoc number, name and number of the segment in the IDoc , the hierarchy and the data
  • The actual data is stored as a string in a field called SDATA, which is a 1000 char long field.

Status Record

  • Status record is attached to an IDoc at every milestone or when it encounter errors.
  • All status record data is stored in EDIDS table.
  • Statuses 1-50 are for outbound while 50-75 for inbound

 

 

 

Outbound IDoc

 

The initial status of an outbound IDoc is 01 and successful status is 30 or 03.

01: IDoc generation successful

30: IDoc is ready to be processed by IDoc Processing job

03: IDoc data is passed to the Port

18: IDoc successfully triggered EDI subsystem

06: IDoc data translated to EDI format

12: IDoc is dispatched successfully to the partner

16: Partner has received the IDoc successfully

 

 

INBOUND IDoc

The initial status of an inbound IDoc is 64 and successful status is 53.

Different validation steps for inbound IDocs are explained below:

50: IDoc received successfully in the system

64: IDoc is ready to be processed by IDoc processing job

53: Application document created and saved successfully. The document number can be found by expanding the status node 53

 

REPROCESSING IDoc

On the basis of IDoc statuses different programs can be used for reprocessing of failed IDocs. These are given below:

 

 

 

 

The SAP IDoc status describes the state of an IDoc at a defined time.

The status for outgoing IDocs has a value range between 01 and 49. The status for incoming IDocs starts at 50.

 

IDoc Types

An IDoc Type, (Basic) defines the structure and format of the business document that is to be exchanged. An IDoc is an instance of an IDoc Type , just like the concept of variables and variables types in programming languages. You can define IDoc types using WE30

What is a Segment?

A Segment defines the format and structure of a data record in IDoc. Segments are reusable components.

For each segment SAP creates

  • Segment Type
  • Segment Definition
  • Segment Documentation

The last 3 characters is the version of the segment

Definitions keep changing as per the version but the segment type remains the same.

Transaction:WE31

 

 

Documentation

Each IDoc are thoroughly documented in transaction WE60

 

Message Type

A message represents a specific type of document that is transmitted between two partners Ex. Orders, orders responses, invoices etc

 

The message type describes the type of business document in an SAP system – e.g., an ORDERS IDoc message type is used to describe — as the name already indicates — an order document. A message type can support different use cases. The message type ORDERS for example can be used for an outbound order (sent to a supplier) or an inbound order (received from a customer).

An IDoc type can be associated with many message types

Also, a message type can be associated with different IDoc types. Transaction WE81

 

Partner Profiles

A partner is defined as a business partner with whom you conduct business and exchange documents

In the partner profile of a partner that we exchange IDocs with, we maintain the parameters that are necessary for exchanging the data. The transaction used is WE20.

 

Port

The port defines the technical characteristics of the connection between your SAP system and the other system you want to transfer data with (subsystem). The port defines the medium in which data is exchanged between the 2 systems.

There are different types of ports. The 2 most commonly used are the TRFC ports used in ALE and File ports which EDI uses.

For TRFC ports we have to give the name of the logical destination created using SM59.

 

IDoc basic type

The IDoc basic type provides the structure for the logical message type. The following image shows the structure of in transaction WE19..

 

The IDoc basic type can manifest itself in multiple logical message types. An ORDERS05 basic type is the foundation for an order (ORDERS), an order change (ORDCHG), an order response (ORDRSP), a quote request (REQOTE) etc.

 

  • EDIDC
    Contains one Control Record per IDoc with one unique IDoc number.
  • EDID4
    Contains one or more Data Records. The reference to the Control Record is done via the unique IDoc number.
  • EDIDS
    Contains one or more Status Records. The reference to the Control Record is done via the unique IDoc number.

 

 

 

Overview

 

Material Master - MATMAS

Customer Master - DEBMAS

Vendor Master - CREMAS

Order - ORDERS

Delivery - DESADV

Invoice - INVOIC

 

 

T-Code : WE02/ WE05/WE07/WE09 –To display IDocs

T-Code : BD10 to send Material Master from sender to receiver.

T-Code : BD12 to send Customer Master from sender to receiver.

T-Code : BD14 to send Vendor Master from sender to receiver.

 

 

For Outbound Messages Number Range is 0 to 49

For Inbound Messages Number Range is 50 and above

 

Success message number in Outbound is  -03

Success message number in Inbound is -53

Failure message in Inbound is -51

 

T-Code : BD87 –to reprocess the data with the same IDoc No.

T-Code : WE19 –to reprocess the data with different IDoc No.

 

 

 

What is ALE ?

ALE stands for Application Link Enabling. Application Link Enabling is a very common technology used in SAP to transfer master and transactional data between different Logical Systems. ALE is a proprietary SAP technology.

 

What is EDI ?

EDI stands for Electronic Data Interchange. EDI is a much older term and has been used in existence much before SAP. EDI is a generic technology that has been standardized using ANSI and EDIFACT standards. EDI is a very standard Industry standard used for business communication across partners.

What is the Difference between EDI & ALE ?

ALE is used for distributing large volumes of similar data between different Logical systems. For example, distributing customer master data between two SAP systems is easily done using ALE. Similarly, when invoices from the core SAP system needs to be transferred to a different non-SAP invoice processing system, ALE is used.

 

EDI is also used to communicate data between different logical systems. For ex., sending out invoices, or order acknowledgements to customers is a perfect example to use EDI. Similarly, we could use EDI to receive inbound orders from customers.

 

There is a general confusion that ALE is a technology to be used between different SAP systems and EDI is the technology to be used between SAP and non-SAP systems.

 

EDI is used to communicate with Partner Systems, while ALE is used to communicate between Logical Systems. Please do however observe that a Partner System could also be a logical system.

 

Regards,

Kamlesh Rampal.

entry.title: Configuring Email SCOT in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
The configuration of email sending in SAP S/4HANA is primarily done through the SCOT transaction (SAPconnect Administration). Here's a breakdown of the key steps involved:

1. Activate the necessary services:

  • Transaction SICF: Ensure that the SAPconnect service is active. You might need to configure the virtual SMTP server here as well.

2. Configure the SMTP node in SCOT:

  • Transaction SCOT: Navigate to Settings -> Outgoing Messages -> SMTP Nodes.
  • Create a new SMTP node or modify the existing one.
  • Provide the following details:
    • Node in use: Check this box to activate the node.
    • Mail host: Enter the hostname or IP address of your SMTP mail server.
    • Mail port: Specify the port number for SMTP (usually 25, 465 for SMTPS, or 587 for submission).
    • Address area: Define the allowed recipient domains (e.g., * for all domains, or specific domains like *.example.com).

3. Set the default domain:

  • In SCOT, go to Settings -> Domain.
  • Enter the default domain for your SAP system (e.g., yourcompany.com). This domain is used for sender addresses when users don't have an explicitly defined email address.

4. Maintain sender addresses:

  • Transaction SU01: For each user who needs to send emails, maintain their email address in the Address tab.

5. Configure send process (optional):

  • In SCOT, you can define how often emails should be sent (e.g., every 5 minutes). This is done by scheduling a background job for the SAPCONNECT program.

6. Test the configuration:

  • Use transaction SO01 or SBWP to create and send a test email.
  • Check the SCOT logs for any errors.

Important notes:

  • Authentication: If your SMTP server requires authentication, you'll need to configure this in SCOT as well.
  • Security: Consider using SMTPS (SMTP over SSL/TLS) for secure email transmission.
  • Troubleshooting: If you encounter issues, check the SCOT logs, the system logs (SM21), and the logs of your SMTP server.

Additional considerations for S/4HANA:

  • Cloud vs. On-Premise: The configuration might vary slightly depending on whether you're using SAP S/4HANA Cloud or On-Premise.
  • SAP Cloud Platform Integration: If you're integrating with other cloud services, you might need to use SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI) for email sending.

This information should help you configure email sending in your SAP S/4HANA system. If you have further questions or need more specific guidance, feel free to ask!

https://www.linkedin.com/posts/mickaelquesnot_scot-activity-7282289862005719040-XEb2?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop

entry.title: How to send out IDocs to Advanced Event Mesh using SAP Application Interface Framework & AIFAEM
entry.content:
Introduction

In my last Blog here I demonstrated how to send out enhanced BOR Events from a ECC or S/4HANA System using AIFAEM. This also can be done with Outbound Idocs. In this Blog I will show this based on the DEBMAS07 IDoc.

 

Prerequisites

  • AIF installed (in S/4 already pre-installed)
  • AIFAEM installed (see my blog referenced above)
  • SM59 REST Destination to AEM prepared (see my blog referenced above)

ALE Setup

ALE Port Definition

If not already existend, you will need a Port Function in WE21 to pass Idocs to AIF -> /AIF/SINGLE_IDOC_PORT_FUNCTION

Partner Profile

I defined the Logical System "AEM" for my Partner Profile ( Transaction SALE -> Define Logical System) and added the Partner Profile in WE20:

Distribution Model

In BD64 I added DEBMAS to my Model View

 

AIF Setup

DDIC Structure Definition

Creating DDIC Object in /AIF/IDOC_GEN

AIF Interface Design

The Previously created ZAIF_DEBMAS07 is the SAP Structure of the AIF Interface. Since it is Outbound, SAP Structure means Source Structure. Target Structure (AIF RAW Structure) for AIFAEM should always consist of CONTROLLER (Structure /AIF/AEM_CONTROLLER) and DATA (actual Payload). 

 

 

 

@EndUserText.label : 'Event Message'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #EXTENSIBLE_ANY
define structure zaif_aem_debmas_event_message {

  controller : /aif/aem_controller;
  data       : zaif_aem_debmas_event;

}

 

 

The Data Component consists of the Cloud Event Header Data and the DEBMAS Idoc as Data

 

 

@EndUserText.label : 'DEBMAS Event'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #EXTENSIBLE_ANY
define structure zaif_aem_debmas_event {

  include /aif/aem_ce_header;
  data : zaif_debmas07;

}

 

 

The Data-Segment could be anything you want to send out. If you decide to have a custom Target Message, you will need to do Field Mappings for the Fields you want to expose. The advantage compared to BOR events is that the Data is already in the Source Message, so no Select on the Database or CDS View is necessary. To keep it simple I assigned the Source Data Type (the Idoc), then the Mapping with Move-Corresponding just passes all the Fields from Source to Target.

AIF Interface Definition

/N/AIF/CUST -> Define Interfaces

AIF needs a Dummy Proxy Outbound Class here, otherwise the Structure Mapping Customizing assumes it is Inbound. After maintaining the class the Transaction might overwrite the RAW Structure, please make sure to have the right one there ( here ZAIF_AEM_DEBMAS_EVENT_MESSAGE). Also there is no Method required since we only the Class as Dummy Entry. This way we also make sure that this interface is not called with any Class method. You can also remove the Class when Structure Mapping is done. Alternative without Class would be: Swap RAW and SAP Structure (RAW Structure = Idoc Structure, SAP Structure = AEM Structure), define Structure Mapping, switch back.

/N/AIF/CUST ->Additional Interface Properties -> Specify Interface Engine

If you are using more than one Interface with this Structure, you might need to maintain the Interface Determination.  /N/AIF/CUST -> System Configuration -> Interface Determination -> Interface Determination For Idoc Interfaces

Structure Mapping

/N/AIF/CUST -> Define Structure Mapping

Source Structure ZAIF_DEBMAS07

Target Structure CONTROLLER

I defined a Global Fix Value here (/N/AIF/CUST -> Define Fix Values) because it is the same for all Interfaces. The Global Fix Value AEM_DEST is explained in my other Blog here

Target Structure DATA

Fix Values:

Field Mappings:

ID, Source and Time are mapped with AIFAEM Function Modules. DATA-EDIDC & DATA-E1KNA1M do have a Sub-Table from the Source Structure assigned, so we can define indirect Mapping & Move-Corresponding.

Source Structure E1KNA1M

Target Structure DATA-DATA-E1KNA1M

Since Source and Target Structure have the same Sub Structures and Fields, we can simply use Move-Corresponding

Source Structure EDIDC

Target Structure DATA-DATA-EDIDC

Since Source and Target Structure have the same Sub Structures and Fields, we can simply use Move-Corresponding

Assign Action

Action has to be in place before (/N/AIF/CUST -> Define Action)

Test

In Transcation BD12 we can send Customers via Idoc

This Message should be returned:

In WE05 we can see the successfull Idoc:

AIF Error Monitoring:

In SAP Cloud Integration I have set up an Iflow to consume this Topic with the AEM Adapter + Log the Payload in the MPL

Connection Parameters can be taken from the AEM Cluster Manager -> Connect -> View by Protocol -> Solace Messaging

Let´s see if the IDoc was consumed:

If I click on Payload I can see the JSON:

in Notepad++:

That´s it!

entry.title: Fundamental concepts for any SAP FICO consultant
entry.content:
Organizational Structure:

✔ Client: The highest level in the SAP system. It represents an independent organizational unit (e.g., a corporation). All other organizational units are assigned to a client.

✔Company Code: The central organizational unit in Financial Accounting (FI). It's an independent legal entity for which complete financial statements can be created (balance sheet, income statement).

✔Controlling Area: The central organizational unit in Controlling (CO). It's used for cost accounting and internal reporting. One or more company codes can be assigned to a controlling area. This is where cost and revenue flow.

✔Business Area: An organizational unit used for internal reporting based on different lines of business or geographical locations within a company code. It's less commonly used in modern SAP implementations, often replaced by profit centers or segments.

Financial Accounting (FI):

🌡 Chart of Accounts (COA): A list of all general ledger (G/L) accounts used by one or more company codes. It defines the structure and numbering of G/L accounts.

🌡 Account Groups: Control the fields that are required, optional, suppressed, or displayed when creating or changing G/L master records. They are defined at the chart of accounts level.

🌡 Fiscal Year Variant: Defines the start and end of the fiscal year and the number of posting periods. It's assigned to the company code.

🌡 Document Types: Classify accounting documents (e.g., invoice, payment, journal entry). They control the number range for documents and which account types can be posted to.

🌡 Posting Keys: Two-digit keys that determine the account type (e.g., G/L account, customer, vendor, asset), the debit/credit indicator, and other posting-relevant information.

🌡 Number Ranges: Define the numbering system for various objects, such as accounting documents, assets, and materials. They prevent duplicate numbers.

🌡 House Banks: The banks with which a company has accounts. Bank master data is maintained here, including account numbers, bank keys, and other relevant information for payment processing.

🌡 Ledger: In S4HANA, ledgers are the central repository for accounting data. The leading ledger (usually 0L) is used for legal reporting, while other ledgers (non-leading ledgers) can be used for parallel accounting according to different accounting principles (e.g., IFRS, US GAAP).

Asset Accounting (AA):

🧯Chart of Depreciation: A list of all depreciation areas used by one or more company codes. It defines the depreciation methods and parameters.

🧯Asset Class: A grouping of assets with similar characteristics (e.g., buildings, machinery, vehicles). It determines the default account assignments, depreciation keys, and other asset-related information.

🧯 Depreciation Area: Represents different valuation methods for assets (e.g., book depreciation, tax depreciation). Each asset can have multiple depreciation areas.

Materials Management (MM) / Controlling (CO) Integration:

✋Valuation Class: Links materials to G/L accounts in FI. It determines which accounts are debited or credited during goods movements.

✋ Material Ledger: In S4HANA, the Material Ledger is mandatory. It collects actual costs for materials and allows for different inventory valuation methods (e.g., moving average price, standard price). It is crucial for parallel valuation and actual costing.


Key Relationships:

Company codes are assigned to a client and a controlling area.

A chart of accounts is assigned to company codes.

A fiscal year variant is assigned to company codes.

A chart of depreciation is assigned to company codes.

Valuation classes link materials in MM to G/L accounts in FI (via account determination).

Understanding these relationships is crucial for configuring and troubleshooting SAP FICO. They represent the foundation of how financial and management accounting data is organized and processed within the system.

https://www.linkedin.com/posts/mickaelquesnot_sap-activity-7281767669370949632-Wln4?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop

entry.title: Testing DRC report with supplier name as 40 characters
entry.content:
Ensuring the reliability of statutory reports is essential for maintaining compliance and data accuracy. The DRC (Digital Reporting Compliance) report is a vital tool in meeting these requirements. This blog focuses on testing the DRC report with a supplier name containing exactly 40 characters, a common edge case that ensures data integrity and proper functionality. 

Why Test with 40-Character Supplier Names? 

A supplier name with 40 characters represents the upper limit for many SAP fields, making it an important test scenario to: 

  1. Validate proper handling of maximum field lengths. 
  1. Ensure no truncation or formatting issues occur. 
  1. Verify accurate execution of invoices and report generation. 

For running a DRC report we need to post an invoice for the specific country for which country we are running DRC and then we should run it. 

Then we will get a document based on the development of DRC. There we could see the documents regarding withholding tax and the supplier name as well. 

There we will be checking supplier names of 40 characters are taking or not. And also, we will get errors for some countries. If we get error due to40 characters, we’ll get that error as well. We will get to know which error is and we can resolve based on the information. 

If we want to post an invoice, obviously we need a supplier / customer. 

For creating supplier, we should go to BP t – code. 

Give necessary details and click on save. 

Now, we have to change BP role to Supplier (Fin. Accounting) 

Add withholding tax type and code. 

Click on save 

Now, copy the supplier's name and go to FB60(Invoice posting) 

 

Click on simulate 

Actually, withholding tax amount would calculate. But here there is not tax rate for the specified tax type and code. And click on save. 

After posting an invoice. We should run the DRC report at the front end. 

For that we must go to front end system and open thus user i.e., AP_MANAGER_(COUNTRY_SPECIFIC) 

 

After logging into the system, the Open run statutory reports app. 

Give specific Report name and Report entity name and click on Go 

 

After clicking on Go, we will get the reports need to be run based on Year or months as well 

We should select recent year or month 

From above, we should open the activity Withholding Tax Reporting 

After that, click on run and give the values for Company codeand click on Run 

We will get Status of In process right after clicking it. 

Basically, we will get the document but due to some errors, we are getting status as Generated with errors 

If we get generated successfully, we could see the documents. In those documents we could see the document and the supplier's name as 40 characters. 

This is how we can test the DRC report with supplier as 40 characters maximum.

entry.title: Highlights of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Release
entry.content:
Welcome to a new era of innovation and seamless integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502! As we usher in 2025, it's the perfect time to celebrate the incredible journey we've shared with our public Cloud ERP community. Over the years, our customers have quickly gained extensive upgrade experience, all thanks to our continuous and seamless feature delivery. Reflecting on this progress, it's astonishing to note that our 33 major SaaS ERP release upgrades are equivalent to encapsulating 130+ years of upgrade experience based on the traditional on-premise deployment models. This showcases the high standard of our SaaS model. With the clean core approach and full lifecycle compliance embedded, we enable customers to harness the latest features instantly.

As our technological landscape continues to evolve, SAP remains at the forefront, driving innovations that empower businesses. I am excited to walk you through my key highlights of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release.

Get ready for an exciting array of updates, including powerful AI enhancements, expanded capabilities of our generative AI co-pilot, Joule, and groundbreaking ERP innovations. Our 2502 release is here to further automate your operations, underpin agility in a cloud-first environment and drive more of your processes towards zero touch. Stay tuned as we dive into these transformative developments.

If you prefer watching rather than reading, you are welcome to check out my product update video: 

Figure 1: Check out my top highlights of the release in this video. 

 

AI-Powered UX Enhancements: Transforming User Interaction with Joule

Joule, our generative AI co-pilot, revolutionizes user experience by streamlining tasks through instant insights and intuitive navigation via natural language input. One notable feature is the new personalized card creation, enabling users to swiftly access vital ERP data such as purchase orders and delivery updates, thus, enhancing decision-making processes.

Figure 2: Get instant insights and swift access to your data with our natural language, generative AI copilot, Joule.

 

 

Our AI-driven UX innovations, AI-assisted smart summarization and AI-assisted easy filter, initially released as beta shipments in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408, have now become generally available. Smart summarization leverages artificial intelligence to condense complex data into easily understandable summaries, saving time and boosting efficiency. Easy filter allows users to apply detailed filters using natural language, simplifying data exploration.

Figure 3: AI-assisted smart summarization saves time and boosts efficiency by condensing complex data into easily understandable summaries.

 

 

Figure 4: AI-assisted easy filter simplifies data exploration and reduces effort.

 

 

As we progress with AI-assisted UX features, the upcoming SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release introduces remarkable advancements in beta testing. These include: 

  • AI-assisted easy enterprise search,
  • AI-assisted smart personalization of My Home,  
  • and AI-assisted error explanation.  

Sign up through this link to start testing.

 

Enhancing Carbon Accountability with SAP Green Ledger

SAP Green Ledger is a transformative financial solution at the heart of carbon accounting. This is designed to integrate seamlessly with connected systems such as ERP and sustainability. By extending the scope of financial accounting to encompass carbon, it applies the principles of double-entry accounting to carbon quantities. These are posted on dimensions relevant to business steering, like profit centers and segments, offering a tangible perspective on carbon impact from provisioning through to delivery. The value proposition of SAP Green Ledger lies in its ability to track, value, and report the carbon footprint of end-to-end operations, with an emphasis on producing auditable and trustworthy reports. By analyzing carbon data alongside financial attributes, it generates insights pivotal for informed decision-making.

 

Figure 5: SAP Green Ledger integrates seamlessly with connected systems such as ERP and sustainability.

 

 

The key capabilities include integration support with other systems, replication of financial master data, importation and enrichment of material movements with carbon information and exporting carbon data for group reporting. Through its robust allocation management and comprehensive visualization, SAP Green Ledger allows businesses to compare carbon data directly with financial data, enhancing strategic understanding and operational transparency.

 

Enhancing Quality Management with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502: Expanding the “Manage Certificate Receipts” App with Create Mode

The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release introduces the create mode in the “Manage Certificate Receipts” app, building on the foundation of innovations introduced in the 2408 release. The original app allowed users to manage and link quality certificates to purchasing documents and goods receipts which improves compliance and traceability. The new Create Mode adds flexibility by enabling users to record certificates received before goods arrive, e.g., by email, and link them to material records upon arrival.

Figure 6: Record certificate receipts before goods arrival, ensuring smoother inventory processes.

 

This enhancement also improves traceability by providing access to existing certificates tied to purchasing documents and items, ensuring complete visibility across processes. Reduce potential error sources and strengthen both transparency and compliance processes with the further evolution of our certificate management capabilities. This enhancement solidifies the Manage Certificate Receipts App as a vital tool for higher efficiency.

 

Unlocking Transformation with GROW with SAP on AWS: A New Era in Cloud ERP

Having your Experience evolution on mind, I´d like to point you to GROW with SAP on AWS, an initiative that seamlessly integrates SAP's undeniable ERP expertise with AWS's expansive cloud infrastructure. With this, SAP is offering transformative solutions for organizations adopting SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. This alliance is a milestone in delivering extraordinary value, tailored for a diverse array of industries, scalability, security, and innovation. GROW with SAP on AWS features a modular design with prebuilt, industry-specific capabilities which allows businesses to scale effortlessly. Rapid deployment is facilitated by proven methodologies such as SAP Activate. Businesses can transition to even swifter deployments.

Figure 7: GROW with SAP on AWS.

 

 

The collaboration on AWS Marketplace simplifies procurement and accelerates adoption, empowering companies to thrive with agility and resilience in today's digital economy. GROW with SAP on AWS emerges beyond ERP, as a transformative enabler that drives the digital transformation frontier forward.

 

Expanding Opportunities with Scalable Solutions: Empowering Partners Through ABAP Cloud Add-Ons

With the general availability of scalable solutions on stack, partners are now equipped to build and extend solutions for their customers, creating significant value and expanding capabilities. This empowerment enables partners to develop, package, and broadly distribute their solutions as ABAP Cloud add-ons, reaching a larger customer base effectively.  Partners begin by developing their extensions within their own landscape and under their own namespace. After thorough testing, the solution is registered and assembled into an installable package. Once published by the partner, it becomes available to customers on the SAP Store. Customers can explore all their purchased and available products using the Discover Product app and can subsequently deploy these solutions in their three-system landscape.

Figure 8: Expand opportunities with scalable solutions.

 

 

This approach greatly complements the existing side-by-side extensibility concept to scale applications according to growing demand, ensuring they stay agile and responsive to rapid market changes. Partners can leverage these capabilities to innovate and differentiate themselves in the competitive market, aligning their offerings closely with customer needs and business requirements as an additional option.

For more information click here. 

 

Advancing Project Efficiency: Innovations in SAP Activate Release

In the ongoing evolution of SAP Activate, we are committed to enhancing our implementation and operational guidance, continuously leveraging insights from customer projects and, in this case, early adopters of Project Lines. Our latest release introduces refined guidance in project management, which enables teams to streamline their workflows and optimize resource allocation. We have also introduced improvements in receiving provisioned environments, making it easier and faster for customers to get started with their projects. Additionally, we have enhanced the identification of activities related to the GROW Adoption Plan, ensuring that users can seamlessly integrate these into their strategic roadmaps.

Another major advancement I’d like to mention is the integration of the “SAP Activate road map viewer” into the “SAP for Me” platform.

Figure 9: SAP Activate Road Map view.

 

 

This transition simplifies access for all users of the comprehensive methodology content of SAP Activate. Seamless learning and efficient project planning are important ingrediencies of a transformed demand with 5 generations of users.

 

Key Summary

In a nutshell, The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release introduces a balanced mix of infrastructure and experience investments, enhanced AI-driven user experiences, embedded AI features, and new or enhanced ERP capabilities, rounded off by the innovative SAP Green Ledger for integrated carbon accounting within financial systems. Additionally, ABAP Cloud Add-Ons give partners an additional tool at hands to extend and differentiate solutions effectively.


Another release that paves the way for businesses to optimize efficiency, scalability, and sustainability in their transformed ERP operations. As all previous releases, it is our invitation to you to join us in the ERP revolution and start your transformative journey. Keep exploring us and keep challenging us to unlock your company's full potential in the evolving digital landscape. Stay tuned for further details in the individual blog posts diving deeper into selected lines of business and industries.

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Feel free to follow me at BeSchulze and on LinkedIn to not miss any updates. 

Yours, Bert

 

📽️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!   

Between January 21 and January 24, we conducted a series of 21 engaging live sessions to showcase the thrilling innovations included in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. If you missed the live sessions, don't worry! You can still access the whole series or select the sessions that interest you the most. Seize this opportunity to watch them on demand at your convenience–anywhere, anytime.  

  

🔎Find All of Our Assets   

 

The release 2502 showcases 185 innovation highlights across the portfolio. Check out our release document for all the details!

Key Highlights of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Release Highlights.

 

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Simplify how users find and share data: new AI features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (2502)
entry.content:
The 2502 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is here and I am excited to share with you how we are helping you achieve more, effortlessly, with new AI capabilities for business users.

These innovations are based on many interactions and feedback sessions we had with customers in 2024, including the SAP Fiori Innovation Day events in Dublin, Chicago, and Heidelberg; as well as the collaboration we had with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customers during the cloud feature beta.  

Filter business data using natural language 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, AI-assisted easy filter enables you to set filters in SAP Fiori elements list report applications using natural language. Rather than setting each selection field manually to drill down for the business data you need, you can write a one-line query summarizing the required information – just like you would ask one of your colleagues. A large language model then interprets this query and translates it into filter values. This simplifies your information search and helps you get to the information you need faster, which can make you more productive and help you focus on the business decisions that matter. For example, imagine a busy sales manager filtering a list of top-performing accounts using a single sentence rather than walking through a variety of filters. 

This can also shorten the training time required to use different applications, as users simply search for what they need rather than needing to memorize specific selection field options.  

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, AI-assisted easy filter allows you to filter business data using natural language

Of course, as the user, you are free to choose how you would like to filter your list report page. Should you wish to switch between natural language filtering and the classic method, that option is available to you. Transparency is also important to us: When you use easy filter, the filter conditions (the values automatically selected for you) are displayed as filter tokens below your query. Based on your results, you can easily try a new query or manually change the filter tokens if you need to make small changes.  

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, AI-assisted easy filter can simplify and streamline how you find business data in your system. Just search for what you need and it will be served up to you. It’s as easy as that!  

Summarize object pages with the help of AI 

Object pages, containing the details of items from list report applications, can be complex. To help you find the information you need easily, you can use SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, AI-assisted smart summarization to generate customizable summaries of SAP Fiori elements-based object pages. Select the sections you want to see and let smart summarization show you what you want to know. This simplifies your search for information and allows you to focus on the details that matter for a given business decision without having to scroll around to find everything you need. 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, AI-assisted smart summarization allows you to generate summaries of SAP Fiori elements object pages

Once you have your summary generated, you can also make edits and share the summary with your colleagues. This simplifies how you share the information you need to move business processes forward.  

Personalize your SAP S/4HANA My Home page with Joule 

You can now use Joule to create cards for the Insights section of your My Home page that will give you personalized, business critical data at a glance. Rather than opening the required app, setting the required filters, and then creating the card natively in your SAP Fiori app; simply tell Joule what you need. It will create a card for you to preview and then you can add it to your My Home page with a single click to get access to essential information right when you log into the system, streamlining your decision making for the day. 

Insights card creation with Joule allows you to query for cards using natural language

Once your card is created, you can open the application directly from the card for further details, if required.  

Please note that this functionality is currently available for the following SAP Fiori applications for the 2502 release: Supplier Invoices List (Version 2), Manage Purchase Orders (Version 2), Manage Sales Orders, Manage Sales Orders - Version 2, Maintain Bill of Material, Find Maintenance Order, Find Maintenance Order and Operation, Manage Purchase Requisitions - Professional, and Project Control - Enterprise Projects. 

“How can I try this out?”  

We’re very excited to get these features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition out to you! Try them out, see how they can help you find, filter, and share information.

To get started, you must agree to the limited promotion terms document to get access to the features. Once you've done that, you're ready to go! 

Jump into one of your SAP-delivered list report applications to try out AI-assisted easy filter. 

Click the SAP Business AI button above the filter bar to use SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, AI-assisted easy filter

While you’re there, drill down into a business object and click the Summarization button at the top of the screen to create a quick summary of the data with AI-assisted smart summarization.  

Click the Summarize button at the top of your object page to use SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, AI-assisted smart summarization

Strike up a conversation with Joule on your My Home page and ask to create a card with data that’s relevant to you.  

Open Joule and ask for your desired business data to be displayed as a card. This can be added to your My Home page with a single click.

Once you’ve tried filtering using natural language, summarizing your object pages, or creating your own cards – we'd love to hear what you think! Feel free to share your feedback in the comments below.  

While we wait for your feedback, we’re also working on additional AI-assisted topics to simplify how you handle error messages in your system, as well as help you create cards for your Insights section directly in My Home. We look forward to sharing them with you. 

Please note that this reflects our current state of planning and SAP reserves the right to change its development priorities at any time.  

A note on data protection in AI solutions from SAP 

We are frequently asked about what happens to your business data once it runs through an AI solution from SAP. Business data and personal information processed by SAP’s AI solutions will not be used​ by third-party LLM providers for training purposes​. If you would like to learn more about our data protection practices with AI solutions, you can refer to SAP's statement on responsible AI:​ https://www.sap.com/products/artificial-intelligence/ai-ethics.html​

entry.title: Basics of sustainability solution SAP Product and REACH Compliance
entry.content:
Introduction

The present blog

This blog will give you a first overview to the functionality of SAP Product and REACH Compliance. This solution is available on SAP ECC as AddOn or technically embedded in SAP S/4HANA. Here you get a first flavor of the scope of the solution.

The author

I have been working since years in the area of sustainability supporting customers in implementing product compliance solutions in several industries.

Overview to previously written blogs:

2022: Reference customers and/or customers success stories for SAP Product and REACH Compliance 

2021: How to uninstall SAP Product and REACH Compliance 2.0 (to get ready for SAP S/4HANA) 

2020: Are you affected by SCIP database of ECHA? Think about SAP Product Compliance for Discrete Ind... 

 

SAP Product and REACH Compliance

Customers

In general, all companies that produce discrete items (articles) might benefit from this solution. Automotive customers can interface the <<International Material Data System>> (IMDS) for downloading material data sheets (MDS) from their suppliers and uploading MDS to their customers.

My customers, where I have implemented SAP Product and REACH Compliance, are active in the following areas: 

  • Automotive (both OEM and Tier-1)
  • Manufacturing (flood control, pump, seal, valve, energy)
  • Hot stamping and coating technology (scope is both process and discrete)
  • Aerospace and Defense
  • Medical Engineering
  • Life Science 

Scope overview to SAP Product and REACH Compliance

The figure below introduces the scope of SAP Product and REACH Compliance. 

Regulatory List Management

Regulatory lists are mainly negative substance lists that might contain listed substances or listed substance groups. A listed substance group is for example "lead" that is being used for <<Restriction of Hazardous Substances>> (ROHS) and that contains more than 700 hundred single listed substances. Negative substance list means that the substances are either prohibited or declarable (or even both). Furthermore, the regulatory list defines for each listed substance the threshold and the algorithm for calculation.

Compliance Checks (in Compliance Requirements)

Most of the Compliance Requirements such as ROHS, IMDS, REACH-SVHC*, etc. are linked with exact one regulatory list and consume the data of them to check whether a basic material, component, product, contains these substances above threshold. This check is embedded in the compliance checks where several check criteria are in that ensure that the <<Compliance Data Object>> (CDO) is compliant or not. These compliance requirements might be linked with one default normalization that is applied for all calculations (even in automatic processing)

* REACH-SVHC: Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation, and restrictions of Chemicals - Substances of Very High Concern

Basic Material Management

For own produced components, often basic materials come into place. They can be added to own components and by this the chemical composition is known as the basic materials contain pure substances**. Using external systems like IMDS basic materials can be downloaded and used in SAP Product and REACH Compliance.

** Pure substances and listed substances are different objects in SAP Product Compliance. For seamless functionality these objects must be linked when existing on both sides.

Compliance Data

SAP Product and REACH Compliance uses its own objects, the compliance data objects. These CDOs are the counterparts of the products, components, supplier-material information, etc. in logistics. Using the Bill-of-Material-Transfer (BOM-Transfer) the system transfers the relevant objects for compliance to product compliance. Where required, high sophisticated logic to retrieve the best variant of a BOM (the best plant using the best usage and alternative) can be applied. These compliance data objects are used for compliance calculation and might have several versions (to still have access to historical data) 

Compliance Declarations from suppliers

Several formats are supported out of the box as "manual data maintenance", IPC1752A (this is a standard from IPC <<Association Connecting Electronics Industries>>), and MDS from IMDS.

Compliance Declarations for customer

Out of the box the system can create MDS to IMDS, IPC1752A format C, and Adobe Interactive Forms documents such as <<Declaration of Substances>> (DoS) and <<Certificate of Compliance>> (CoC).

Compliance Reporting

Here the system offers <<Core Data Services>> (CDS) views to retrieve data using compliance analysis from the system. Besides that, Excel templates are available to retrieve data from selected products or components and transfer them to Excel.

SAP Product and REACH Compliance - scope overview

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Deeper insight to BOM Transfer

As we have already touched above, the BOM Transfer copies the relevant objects from logistics to product compliance. In case the objects do not yet exist, they are created, otherwise the system updates them.

Objects

  • Materials (system will identify them and categorize them as "package", "bulk", or "product or product part"
  • BOM Structure (system uses specification database to store this information)
  • Supplier-material objects (these supplier parts are identified for most cases via source list or purchasing info record)

BOM Transfer triggers

As the figure below shows, there are certain triggers possible to initiate this transfer.

Manual transfer

Manually started by the user. That is what SAP has foreseen for trading goods. It might help during cutover, too. But for most use cases the customers want to have a higher automatization.

Bill of Material gets created or changed

That is also the SAP standard approach, however; the used workflows are not appreciated by customers and often a BOM gets changes to a relevant status before the material gets a relevant status. So, most customers rely on our own worklist that covers several use cases.

Supplier material links have changed

This is an appreciated SAP standard approach. Once the source list or purchasing info record gets created or deleted, the single material transfer is initiated by the system.

Material has changed

Not part of SAP standard functionality. But while applying the functionality of worklist from above we can handle this.

BOM Transfer - triggers

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Applying filters for BOM Transfer

To emphasize the powerfulness of BOM Transfer we shed some light to the filter criteria. Using two existing structures, for the materials, the BOM header, and the BOM positions filters might be configurated. By doing this we only get relevant data into product compliance. Further configuration might be used for BOM alternative handling. Last but not least, there are user exits available that allow us to realize special customer requirements, e.g., to not overwrite weight data in case a manual flag is set (weight data is always a challenge...)

Degrees of freedom using BOM Transfer

There exist two poles.

Pole 1 is to transfer each and every alternative into product compliance. This ensures maximal compliance; however, leads to complexity. Some use cases do not permit this approach. 

Pole 2 is to transfer only one structure to product compliance. This might be the latest changed BOM or the BOM with the smallest alternative.

Often a realistic scenario (using different plant nodes) gets implemented as the figure below shows.

BOM Transfer - degrees of freedom

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Product Compliance structure

The product compliance structure is being displayed in the system and used by the system for the compliance calculation. 

Objects in Product Compliance structure

The figure below provides an overview of (some of) the objects being used.

Product Compliance Structure - objects

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Normalizations

Normalizations provide a special view to the product compliance structure. So you might see all leaf elements, only basic materials, elements with issues only, etc. then normalizations come into the game. 

Beyond that there exists plenty of uses cases where normalizations get applied to manipulate the product compliance structure. The figure below shows one use case: the system creates for a procured component supplier parts. For these three suppliers you have instructed them to use exact basic material for steel XYZ. The suppliers won't give any compliance information to you. You plug-in the basic material to the component. Now the system considers both the basic material and the supplier parts and states "missing data". With the "prefer basic material" normalization, the supplier parts get suppressed in case there exists a basic material.

Product Compliance Structure - normalizations

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Summary

SAP Product and REACH Compliance is a powerful tool. For my customers I have created process documentations with 200+ pages, for sure this cannot be added into a blog. However, the basic concept on a high level is given.

In case of questions or required help just contact the author.

entry.title: How can SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite support your journey to SAP S/4HANA Cloud?
entry.content:
Yesterday, while presenting SAP’s financial results for FY2024, our CEO Christian Klein explained that we exceeded our cloud goals, accelerating cloud revenue and current cloud backlog growth against a much larger base. He also mentioned that half of our cloud order entry are now including AI. Clearly, our aim is to provide our customers with a suite of cloud-based applications that provides the most valuable data in business and powers unique AI capabilities. In this blog post, I would like to present how SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite – both running on SAP Business Technology Platform – are at the heart of SAP’s strategy and support our customers in their journey from legacy ERPs to SAP S/4HANA Cloud.


We know that for many SAP customers, the transition to SAP S/4HANA can be challenging, because someone in the organization has added a lot of custom code to your ERP 😉. That’s why SAP has created RISE with SAP, a packaged offering that helps you transform on-premise SAP ERP systems, modernize business processes, and become more agile in the cloud. And in RISE with SAP, you will find anything you need to fulfill your needs.

Let me be a bit more specific: every company in the world uses software to run their operations. We all know the most efficient and effective way is to use standard software like our SAP S/4HANA ERP. At the same time, we are fully aware that every organization in the world is unique and gains competitive advantage through their very specific processes. This might be the way they run processes for their customers because they offer unique experiences, or the way they engage with their suppliers because they may be faster in the supply chain processes, or it might be their HR processes which allows them to retain the best talent in the market… whatever it is, that makes your company unique, it should be reflected in your software because no one likes manual processes in 2025. In the context of migrating from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, following the Clean Core strategy is a powerful opportunity: by eliminating custom code, technical debt, and standardizing the processes and data that underpin ERP operations, your business will benefit from more best practices while being able to customize what it needs to differentiate itself. And when it comes to standardization to achieve clean core with proper extensions and integrations, SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite on SAP BTP enter the game!

SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite add great value to your Cloud ERP

SAP Build allows you to extend and personalize SAP S/4HANA to meet your needs. It accelerates development and automation of extensions with low-code, pro-code, and generative AI tools. It enables stable, secure, and scalable ERP extensions that adhere to a prescribed extensibility model, with compliance to code quality standards and best practices. Tailored for every SAP customer, any size, any landscape, any industry, SAP Build is the best of suite tools for developers and key users collaborating in AI-powered ERP extension development.

Let me say it simply: SAP Build adds great value to your Cloud ERP. You will get more out of your cloud ERP investment with SAP Build. Just because it lets you easily customize, extend, integrate and automate business processes in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud solution to meet specific requirements while keeping the core clean. Watch our webinar “Extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Build: your Free Toolkit”: in an hour, you will discover how you can leverage SAP Build’s low-code, pro-code, and generative AI development tools to drive innovation, automate processes, and supercharge your S/4HANA Cloud experience.

SAP Integration Suite is an iPaaS that helps you quickly integrate on-premises and cloud-based processes, services, applications, events, and data. For sure, it also adds value to your ERP: it connects business processes with proven pre-built integrations, APIs, connectors, and best practices. It provides upgrade-stable interfaces, proper monitoring and error resolution capabilities, and actively used and well-documented connectors.

For What Use Cases?

Let me give you very concrete examples where SAP Build is very helpful to address specific challenges in your Lead to Cash processes:

  • Digitize in-store cash sales processing
  • Automate the creation of sales orders from Excel files
  • Automate mass maintenance of billing blocks
  • Automate the approvals of return requests
  • Automate the creation of sales orders from unstructured data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Automate managing credit blocks on sales orders

and more…

I can also share many use cases that could improve your Source to Pay processes:

  • Centralize and streamline supplier communications with dedicated business sites
  • Mass maintenance of purchase contracts
  • Automate the creation of purchase requisitions from structured data in SAP S/4HANA
  • Automate the processing of paper-based invoices
  • Automatically populate missing material codes in purchase requisitions
  • Track sustainability data across your supply chain

and more…

You will find complementary use cases on our SAP Build web page.

And there is more: on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, you can browse 500+ pre-built content packages for SAP Build and 600+ pre-built content packages for SAP Integration Suite. If you have developed custom code in your on-premises ERP in the past to address such use cases or specific needs, you can explore this vast catalog to see if some of these packages might be a good fit for your cloud ERP migration process. Indeed it is very important to know that SAP Build comes with pre-built content for ECC: you can use these SAP Build packages on ECC already to standardize some extensions, and then all the extensions developed on ECC with SAP Build will taken to S/4HANA during the migration.

Insights from Customers

What I presented above is not only theoretical: customers who have been through this SAP S/4HANA journey share valuable insights that can really help you. I have selected two few interesting stories, in different industries.

Sony Pictures

Let me start with Sony Pictures Networks: relying on the legacy system, SAP ECC, their highly customized and siloed infrastructure struggled to align with the company's integrated business processes. And decentralized data presented SAP challenges in consolidating business reports until the end.

Sony decided to remaster their operations for the future through next-generation ERP, with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, and Enterprise Automation, combining SAP Signavio, SAP Build And SAP Integration Suite. Indeed, SAP Signavio radically accelerates business process transformation, and I engage you to read this blog post explaining how customers on SAP ECC systems can prepare for an SAP S/4HANA transformation with SAP Signavio.

“By migrating to SAP S/4HANA and adopting SAP Business Technology Platform, we can keep the core clean. This strategy allows for faster development of any new technologies or any new process rollouts and enables us to take advantage of generative AI as part of our way forward” explains Mahesh Patil, SVP and Head of IT – Infrastructure, Integration, and Data Lake, Sony Pictures Networks India. And the results are excellent:

  • 100% automated process mining, reducing effort from weeks to days
  • 50% of interfaces managed through SAP Integration Suite
  • ~85% faster mobile app creation, reducing effort from four weeks to three days

Hitachi

Would you like another good example? Let’s take Hitachi High-Tech Corporation: they replaced their highly customized multinational business landscape by investing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud in a two-tier public and private cloud model. With a fit-to-standard ethos, the company can now focus on supporting business innovation across its subsidiaries using a side-by-side approach on SAP Business Technology Platform.

“Our new policy is to fit our business processes to standard as much as possible, reducing our need for customizations” says Yuri Yasuda, Manager Corporate DX Department, Digital Transformation Business Group, Hitachi High-Tech Corporation, who shares impressive KPIs:

  • 94% reduction in customization footprint
  • 18x faster handling of solution updates
  • 100% of on-site maintenance requirements eliminated

You can also find inspiration in the S/4HANA projects that Mahindra Group, PX Energy, LEOCH, Farys, Delta Light or Prestige have successfully implemented.

Start your S/4HANA journey with the support of SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite!

I hope that you’re now convinced that SAP BTP in general and SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite in particular are very powerful to ease your migration to SAP S/4HANA. To start this journey, watch our short video and request a customized demo: together, let’s make your business unstoppable!

entry.title: Principle of Reorder Point Planning
entry.content:
The basis of reorder point planning is the comparison of the available MRP stock
(total from plant stock and the fixed receipts) with the reorder point. If the available
stock is less than the reorder point, then procurement is triggered.

Reorder Point Planning

The reorder point should cover the expected average material requirements during
the replenishment lead time.

The safety stock should cover both excess material consumption during the
replenishment lead time and the additional consumption caused by delayed deliveries.

The safety stock is therefore part of the reorder point.

The reorder point is composed of the expected average material requirements during
the replenishment lead time and the safety stock. You must therefore consider the
following points when defining the reorder point:

• Safety stock
• Previous consumption or future requirements
• Replenishment lead time

During automatic reorder point planning, you determine the reorder point and the
safety stock in the material master. During automatic reorder point planning, the
integrated forecast program determines the reorder point and safety stock.
The reorder point is calculated as follows: reorder point = safety stock + daily
requirement * lead time.

Inventory management continuously monitors the plant stock during reorder point
planning. At each material withdrawal, the system checks whether the withdrawal
falls short of the reorder point. If this is the case, an entry is created in the planning
file for the next planning run. In the same way, the system checks whether the plant
stock still falls short of the reorder point during a material return. If so, a planning
flag is created so that the planning run can delete superfluous procurement proposals.

If return deliveries become superfluous as a result of fixed scheduled receipts, the
planning run proposes that these receipts are deleted. In this case, the MRP controller
must check with Purchasing whether the purchase order can be retracted.
The system calculates net requirements during the planning run.

entry.title: LSMW with IDOC Method (MATMAS Example)
entry.content:
This outlines using LSMW with the IDOC method to migrate material master data (MATMAS) where IDOCs are the source.

1. Introduction:

  • LSMW (Legacy System Migration Workbench): A standard SAP tool for data migration from legacy systems or external sources.
  • IDOC (Intermediate Document): A standard SAP format for asynchronous communication between systems. In this case, your external system generates MATMAS IDOCs.

2. Scenario: Migrating Material Master Data (MATMAS) using IDOC as Source:

  • Assumption: An external system creates MATMAS IDOCs containing the material master data to be migrated.

3. LSMW Project Setup:

  • Project: A container for all migration activities. Give it a descriptive name (e.g., "MATMAS_MIGRATION").
  • Subproject: A subdivision within a project (e.g., "MATMAS_IDOC_IN").
  • Object: The SAP object to be created/changed (e.g., "0050" for Material Master).

4. Object Attributes:

  • Choose the "IDOC" processing method.

5. IDOC Configuration:

  • Basic Type: Specify the MATMAS IDOC basic type (e.g., MATMAS05). This defines the structure of the IDOC.
  • Message Type: The message type associated with the basic type (e.g., MATMAS).

6. Source Structure:

  • Define the source structure(s) to match the segments of your MATMAS IDOC. Each segment in the IDOC will correspond to a source structure in LSMW. Name these structures descriptively (e.g., "E1MARM", "E1KNMT", etc.). These structures mirror the IDOC segments you'll be receiving.

7. Source Fields:

  • For each source structure, define the fields that correspond to the fields within the IDOC segment. Map these to the fields in the IDOC structure. Pay close attention to data types and lengths.

8. Structure Relations:

  • Define the relationships between the source structures. This mirrors the hierarchical structure of the IDOC. For MATMAS, you'll likely have a main segment (e.g., E1MARM) and dependent segments (e.g., E1KNMT, E1MVKM, etc.).

9. Field Mapping and Conversion Rules:

  • Map the source fields to the target SAP fields. This is where you define how the data from the IDOC will be transferred to the material master.
  • Implement any necessary conversion rules (e.g., data type conversions, value mappings, calculations). ABAP code can be used for complex logic.

10. Create Processing Program:

  • LSMW generates an ABAP program that processes the IDOCs.

11. IDOC File:

  • The IDOCs from your external system need to be available to LSMW. This can be done by storing them in a file on the application server or by using a file port.

12. Read Data:

  • LSMW reads the IDOC data from the file.

13. Convert Data:

  • LSMW converts the data according to your mapping rules.

14. Generate Batch Input Session (or Direct Input/BAPI):

  • LSMW creates a batch input session (or if configured, uses Direct Input or BAPI) to create/update the material master records in SAP. IDOC processing typically uses batch input under the hood.

15. Process Batch Input Session:

  • Run the batch input session to create/update the material master data.

16. Verification:

  • Thoroughly check the created/updated material master records in SAP to ensure the data was migrated correctly. Use standard SAP transactions (e.g., MM03) to verify.

Key Considerations:

  • IDOC Structure: Understanding the MATMAS IDOC structure is crucial.
  • Error Handling: Implement robust error handling to manage any issues during IDOC processing. LSMW provides logging and error analysis tools.
  • Testing: Test the migration thoroughly in a development or test environment before migrating data to production.
  • Performance: For large volumes of data, consider optimizing the LSMW project and using appropriate hardware resources.

This detailed breakdown should provide a comprehensive guide to using LSMW with IDOCs for MATMAS migration. Remember to consult SAP documentation for specific details and best practices.
